Dear Mr. Haas: 
Thank you for your recent letter requesting supplemental 
ice on our advice letter to Peter E. Tracy. 
T~at letter is 
incorporated herein by reference. 
You ask: 
Would the fact that a councilman in a general law 
city must approve or reject all demands for the 
payment of money (Government Code Section 37202) 
change your advice? 
First, your attention is directed specifically to the footnote 
on page 2 of the Tracy letter. 
Secondly, under the Political 
Reform Ace, any conflict, wnich might otherwise arise under the 
provisions which you point out, could be avolded by the council-
memoer by disqualifying himself/herself from participating in 
t~e decision to approve or reJect the demand ror the money. 
I truse tnat this letter satisfactorily responds co your 
question. 
Should you have any question aoout it, please call me 
at 916/322-5901.

sections.conclusion: Under the Political Reform Act, any conflict which might otherwise arise under the provisions which you point out, could be avoided by the council-member by disqualifying himself/herself from participating in the decision to approve or reject the demand for the money.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a councilman's mandatory duty to approve or reject all payment demands under Government Code Section 37202 affects FPPC's prior advice regarding conflicts of interest.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A councilman can avoid conflicts of interest by disqualifying himself or herself from participation in decisions to approve or reject payment demands, in accordance with the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is FPPC's response letter dated December 27, 1982 to Thomas Haas regarding supplemental advice on letter A-82-150. References prior advice letter to Peter E. Tracy (A-82-203). OCR quality is moderate with some minor artifacts. Incoming request letter from Haas dated December 10, 1982 is also included in the document.

embedding.summary: FPPC counsel responds to supplemental inquiry from Fairfield City Attorney regarding whether a general law city councilman's statutory duty to approve/reject payment demands affects prior advice on conflict of interest matters. The response indicates conflicts can be avoided through self-disqualification under the Political Reform Act.
